plain_definition: An internal compliance framework a business maintains to identify, prevent, and report transactions that would violate US economic sanctions -- typically including screening counterparties and transactions against sanctions lists, and the technical capability to block, freeze, or reject a transaction that would breach a sanctions rule. Under the GENIUS Act, a permitted payment stablecoin issuer must maintain an effective sanctions compliance program, with the program's required elements set out in OFAC's implementing rules.
formal_definition: The GENIUS Act (Public Law 119-27) directs that a permitted payment stablecoin issuer maintain an effective sanctions compliance program. OFAC's implementing proposal (Federal Register Doc. 2026-06963, published 10 April 2026) would add a new part to 31 CFR Chapter V setting the program's required elements, including technical capability to block, freeze, or reject transactions and to comply with lawful orders to seize, freeze, burn, or prevent transfer of stablecoins.
